message:
fix(net): always re-arm reactor registrations after oneshot events

The polling crate uses oneshot-style notifications: after an event fires,
the reactor registration is disarmed and must be explicitly re-armed via
modify() before new events will be delivered. The previous code skipped
set_interest() when the interest flags hadn't changed, which silently
dropped future wakeups after the first event.

Affected I/O primitives (all register_interest paths):
- TcpListener: add set_interest() re-arm before update_waker check
- TcpStream: remove combined != existing guard around set_interest()
- OwnedReadHalf / OwnedWriteHalf: same guard removal
- UdpSocket: same guard removal
- UnixListener: add set_interest() re-arm before update_waker check
- UnixStream: restructure to call set_interest() before update_waker
- UnixDatagram: same guard removal

All paths retain the NotConnected -> drop registration -> wake fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
